src package

Submodules

src.flask_app module

src.flask_app.create_app(test_config=None, pipeline_runner=None)[source]

Flask application factory for the Grad Cafe Analytics system.

Constructs and configures the Flask application, setting up database connections and registering routes. It supports dependency injection for testing purposes, allowing for mocked configurations and pipeline runners.

Parameters:

  • test_config (dict, optional) – Optional mapping of configuration values to override the defaults for testing.

  • pipeline_runner (callable, optional) – Optional callable to override the default ETL pipeline execution. Used for injecting mocked runners during testing.

Returns:

The configured Flask application instance ready to serve requests.

Return type:

flask.Flask

src.flask_app.default_pipeline_runner(app_instance)[source]

Executes the full Grad Cafe data pipeline: scraping, cleaning, and loading.

This function runs as a background thread to prevent blocking the Flask application. It executes the scrape.py, clean.py, and load_data.py scripts sequentially using the subprocess module. It manages the IS_SCRAPING state flag to lock UI elements during execution.

Parameters:

app_instance (flask.Flask) – The current Flask application instance containing the configuration.

Raises:

  • subprocess.CalledProcessError – If any of the underlying ETL scripts fail during execution.

  • Exception – For any other unhandled errors during pipeline execution.

src.inspect_data module

src.inspect_data.inspect_data(filepath)[source]

Loads the JSON dataset and validates numeric values. Identifies potential data entry errors or scraping artifacts that fall outside of standard logical ranges.

src.load_data module

src.load_data.clean_numeric(val, min_val, max_val)[source]

Sanitizes and validates numeric data scraped from the web.

Attempts to convert a raw string or numeric input into a float. Enforces logical minimum and maximum boundaries to filter out user-entry errors and scraping artifacts.

Parameters:

  • val (str, float, or None) – The raw input value to be sanitized (e.g., from a JSON field).

  • min_val (float) – The absolute minimum acceptable limit for the value.

  • max_val (float) – The absolute maximum acceptable limit for the value.

Returns:

The sanitized float if within bounds, or None if invalid/out-of-bounds.

Return type:

float or None

src.load_data.get_db_connection()[source]

Establishes and returns a connection to the PostgreSQL database.

Retrieves the database connection string from the ‘DATABASE_URL’ environment variable. Defaults to a local test configuration if not found.

Returns:

An active PostgreSQL connection instance.

Return type:

psycopg.Connection

src.load_data.load_data(json_path='src/web_scraping/applicant_data.json')[source]

Loads the standardized applicant JSON dataset into PostgreSQL.

This function handles the extraction of JSON data, connects to the database, and executes the table creation (if missing). It sanitizes all fields using boundary constraints and handles date formatting. Idempotency is guaranteed via an ‘ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING’ unique constraint applied to the applicant’s result URL.

Parameters:

json_path (str) – The file path to the target JSON data. Defaults to the main scraped dataset.

Raises:

  • FileNotFoundError – If the specified JSON data file does not exist.

  • Exception – If a database transaction fails or schema constraints are violated.

src.query_data module

src.query_data.get_db_connection()[source]

Establishes and returns a connection to the PostgreSQL database.

Retrieves the database connection string from the ‘DATABASE_URL’ environment variable to allow seamless switching between testing and production environments.

Returns:

An active PostgreSQL connection instance.

Return type:

psycopg.Connection

src.query_data.get_metrics()[source]

Executes analytical SQL queries against the applicants dataset.

This function runs a comprehensive suite of queries to calculate overall applicant volume, demographic percentages, average standardized test scores, and acceptance rates. It prints a formatted report directly to the terminal and bundles the raw data into a dictionary for rendering in the web application.

Returns:

A dictionary containing the aggregated metrics for all 11 analytical questions.

Return type:

dict

Raises:

Exception – If a database connection fails or a SQL query encounters an error.
